About Umple

History, team, and research behind Model-Oriented Programming

What is Umple?

Umple is a modeling tool and programming language family that enables Model-Oriented Programming. It adds abstractions such as associations, attributes, and state machines to object-oriented programming languages such as Java, Python, C++ and PHP.

Umple creates reliable code from highly-readable abstractions. The resulting system has many fewer lines than if programmed in a typical programming language, since developers avoid having to write boilerplate code for constructors, getters, setters, association management, and state machine logic.

Umple has also been found to help students learn to design software rapidly in the classroom.

Origin of the Name

The word "Umple" is a play on words, meaning "Simple", "UML Programming Language" and "Ample":

Simple
Umple is intended to be simple from the programmer's perspective because there is less code to write and fewer degrees of freedom than either Java or UML. Code that is eliminated includes boilerplate for adding, deleting, and modifying links of associations, as well as constructors and methods for accessing attributes.
UML Programming Language
Umple adds key features of UML to its target languages -- namely, association constructs and the simplification of attribute declarations.
Ample
Despite the restrictions imposed by the deliberate simplicity of Umple, it is intended to have sufficient power to program the functional layer of most kinds of systems.

Team

Umple is led by Dr. Timothy Lethbridge, professor in the School of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, Faculty of Engineering at the University of Ottawa. Much of the development has been by graduate and undergraduate students at uOttawa.

The project receives support from the Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council of Canada (NSERC) and the Digital Research Alliance of Canada.

👥 Developers View contributors on GitHub 🎓 Capstone Students University capstone project participants

Research Publications

Umple has been the subject of extensive academic research, with publications covering model-driven development, code generation, state machines, UML, and software engineering education.

Publications List Complete list of Umple-related academic papers Mendeley Group Curated collection on Mendeley

Philosophy

Umple embodies the philosophy that modeling and programming should be seamlessly integrated rather than treated as separate activities. By embedding modeling constructs directly in programming languages, Umple allows developers to benefit from the abstraction power of UML while retaining the expressiveness and tooling of their preferred language.

Read more about the Umple philosophy

Community

Join the Umple community through any of these channels:
